title: Razorpay Bracket Balance Checker
description:
Razorpay ke webhook payload validator mein developer ne config file likhi hai jismein round, square aur curly brackets use hote hain. Tujhe ek string `s` di hai jo letters aur brackets mix mein contain karti hai. Return `true` agar saare brackets properly nested aur matched hain, warna `false`.

Non-bracket characters ko ignore kar — sirf bracket validity check karni hai.

examples.explanation: Saare brackets pair-wise matched aur properly nested hain.

constraints: s mein lowercase letters aur brackets `()[]{}` ho sakte hain
hints: Stack mein sirf opening brackets push kar.
Closing aaye toh top check kar — match nahi hua toh false.
Edge case: empty string truthy hai, end pe stack non-empty toh false.

title: Swiggy Next Bigger Order Value
description:
Swiggy delivery partner ke pass aaj din bhar ke order values ka array hai. Har order ke liye batao agle kisi order ki value strictly badi hai ki nahi — agar hai toh wo value, warna `-1`. Return same-length array of answers.

Yahaan 'agla' matlab right side index pe.

examples.explanation: Index 0 ke baad pehla bigger 5 hai, index 1 ke baad bhi 5, index 2 ke baad koi nahi, etc.
Decreasing array — kisi ke aage bigger nahi.

hints: Brute force O(n^2) — har element ke liye linear scan.
Monotonic stack of indices use kar — decreasing order maintain.
Jab current element top se bada ho toh pop karke answer fill kar.

title: Zomato Rating Rise Wait Days
description:
Zomato ke restaurant owner ko daily ratings milti hain. Ek array `ratings` di hai jismein har din ka rating hai. Har din ke liye batao kitne din ka wait karna padega ek strictly higher rating ke liye. Agar future mein higher nahi hai toh `0` return kar.

Return integer array same length.

examples.explanation: Day 0 (3) -> day 1 (4) is higher. Day 1 (4) -> day 3 (5). Day 3 (5) — koi higher nahi.

hints: Days ka difference chahiye, isiliye stack mein indices store kar.
Monotonic decreasing stack — jab current rating top se badi ho toh pop karke gap nikaal.
Jo end tak unmatched rahe unka answer 0.

title: Flipkart Revenue Largest Rectangle
description:
Flipkart product analytics dashboard mein bar chart hai jahaan har bar ki height us product ki daily revenue hai aur width 1 unit. Tujhe largest rectangular area find karni hai jo continuous bars ko cover kare.

Return integer — maximum rectangle area.

examples.explanation: Heights [5,6] ka rectangle width 2 height 5 = area 10.
Best rectangle: height 4 width 1, ya height 2 width 2.

hints: Har bar ke liye kitni left-right tak extend kar sakta hai usi height pe — wo nikalna hai.
Monotonic increasing stack of indices — jab smaller aaye toh pop karke area calc kar.
Sentinel 0 array end mein add kar takki saare leftover stack pop ho jayein.

title: Paytm Reverse Polish Calculator
description:
Paytm scientific calculator backend reverse polish notation use karta hai expressions ko evaluate karne ke liye. Tujhe tokens ka array diya hai jismein integers aur `+`, `-`, `*`, `/` operators hain. Expression evaluate karke integer return kar.

Division truncates towards zero.
constraints: Har token ya valid integer hai (signed) ya operator
Intermediate results aur final answer 32-bit integer mein fit
hints: Stack pe operands push kar.
Operator dikhe toh top do pop kar — order matter karta hai (a op b).
Division truncate towards zero: `Math.trunc(a/b)` ya negative-aware logic.

title: CRED Decode Promo Code String
description:
CRED ke promo engine encoded strings store karta hai jaise `3[ab]` jiska matlab `ababab`. Encoding rule: `k[encoded_string]` jahaan k positive integer hai. Nesting allowed hai jaise `3[a2[c]]` = `accaccacc`.

Decoded string return kar.
constraints: s mein lowercase letters, digits aur square brackets `[]` honge
Original aur decoded string length 1e5 se kam
hints: Two stacks: ek count ke liye, ek 'previous string' ke liye.
`[` pe push current state, `]` pe pop karke repeat.
Multi-digit numbers ko handle kar — `k = k*10 + digit`.

title: Uber Ride Cancellation Adjacent Cleaner
description:
Uber dispatch log mein consecutive same characters cancellation indicate karte hain. Tujhe ek string `s` di hai. Repeatedly do adjacent equal characters ko remove kar jab tak koi adjacent pair na bache.

Final string return kar.

examples.explanation: xx remove -> azzy -> zz remove -> ay.

hints: Stack pe characters push kar — top se same hai toh pop kar de.
End mein stack ko join karke string banadi.
Cascading removals automatic ho jaate hain stack se.

title: PhonePe Min Stack Operations
description:
PhonePe analytics module ek special stack chahiye jismein push/pop/top ke saath har waqt minimum value bhi O(1) mein milti rahe.

Tujhe operations ka 2D array diya hai. Har operation `["push", x]`, `["pop"]`, `["top"]`, `["min"]` mein se ek hai. Return karna hai array — har operation ke liye result (push/pop ke liye `null`, top/min ke liye number).
constraints: Pop/top/min sirf non-empty stack pe call honge
hints: Parallel min-stack rakh — har push ke saath current min track kar.
Pop dono se kar.
Min stack ka top hamesha overall min.

title: Swiggy Peak Orders Window
description:
Swiggy ka surge pricing algorithm last `k` minutes ke maximum order count pe depend karta hai. Tujhe orders array `a` diya hai (per-minute counts) aur window size `k`. Har sliding window ka maximum return kar.

Return array of length `n - k + 1`.
hints: Naive O(n*k) too slow — har window ka max scan.
Monotonic decreasing deque of indices use kar.
Front se out-of-window indices remove, back se smaller-than-current pop, current push.

title: Razorpay Recent Requests Counter
description:
Razorpay rate-limiter ko track karna hai pichle 3000 milliseconds (inclusive) mein kitne API requests aaye. Tujhe timestamps ka non-decreasing array `t` diya hai. Har timestamp ke baad return kar count of requests within `[t[i]-3000, t[i]]`.

Return array same length as `t`.

examples.explanation: Pichle 3000 ms ke liye respective counts.
Saare close hain — count grows linearly.

hints: Queue use kar — push timestamps, front se old wale (< t[i]-3000) pop kar.
Queue size hi answer hai.
Amortized O(1) per request.

title: Atlassian Two Stacks To Queue
description:
Atlassian Jira ke task scheduler ko FIFO queue chahiye but library mein sirf stacks hain. Two-stack technique se queue simulate kar.

Operations: `["enqueue", x]`, `["dequeue"]`, `["peek"]`. Return array — har op ke liye result (enqueue null, dequeue/peek ka value, empty pe null).
hints: Do stacks: `inSt` for incoming, `outSt` for outgoing.
Dequeue/peek pe agar `outSt` empty hai toh `inSt` se saare pour kar (reverse order ho jata hai).
Amortized O(1) per operation.

title: Meesho Two Queues To Stack
description:
Meesho seller dashboard mein LIFO behavior chahiye but environment mein sirf queues mile. Single queue (or two) se stack simulate kar.

Operations: `["push", x]`, `["pop"]`, `["top"]`. Return array — push ke liye `null`, pop/top ke liye value (empty pe null).
hints: Single queue trick: push pe element add karke saare older elements ko rotate kar.
Naya element queue ke front pe aa jata hai — pop/top easy.
Push O(n), pop/top O(1).

title: Google Keyboard Backspace Typing Match
description:
Google search bar mein typing simulator — `#` character backspace represent karta hai. Do typed strings `s` aur `t` di hain. Return `true` agar dono ka final result equal hai, warna `false`.

Empty text par backspace karne se kuch nahi hota.
constraints: s aur t mein lowercase letters aur '#' honge
hints: Stack par characters push, '#' aaye toh pop.
Final stacks compare kar.
Empty stack pe pop no-op hota hai.

title: Flipkart Truck Convoy Collision
description:
Flipkart logistics mein highway pe trucks chal rahe hain. Array `a` mein har element ek truck ka size hai — positive matlab right ki taraf, negative matlab left ki taraf. Same speed sab. Jab opposite-direction trucks meet karte hain, smaller one destroy ho jata hai. Equal size ho toh dono destroy.

Final trucks left in original order return kar.
hints: Stack mein currently surviving trucks rakh.
Negative truck aaye toh stack ke top (positive) trucks se compare karke pop kar jab tak chhote nahi mil jaate.
Equal sizes — dono ko remove kar (one pop, current 'die').

title: Mumbai Monsoon Building Rain Trap
description:
Mumbai mein monsoon ke time buildings ke beech paani jam ho jata hai. Tujhe heights array `h` di hai (each width 1). Calculate kar total kitna paani trap hota hai bars ke beech.

Return integer.
hints: Monotonic decreasing stack maintain — jab taller bar aaye toh basin form hota hai.
Pop karke trapped water = (min(left, right) - bottom) * width.
Width = currentIdx - newTop - 1.

title: CRED Filesystem Path Simplify
description:
CRED ke build pipeline mein Unix-style file paths normalize karne hain. Tujhe absolute path string `p` di hai (starts with `/`). Simplify kar:
- `.` current directory (skip).
- `..` parent directory (one level up).
- Multiple consecutive `/` ek hi treat hota hai.

Return canonical path (starts with single `/`, no trailing `/` except root).
hints: Path ko `/` se split kar.
Stack pe valid directory names push kar — '..' aaye toh pop.
End mein `/` se join kar prepended `/`.

title: Amazon Iterative Binary Tree Preorder
description:
Amazon ke catalog mein category tree hai — recursion stack overflow se bachne ke liye iterative DFS chahiye. Tree ko `left` aur `right` arrays se represent kiya hai jahaan `left[i]` node `i` ka left child index hai (`-1` means none), similarly `right`. Root index `root` diya hai.

Return preorder traversal (root, left, right) ke node indices ka array. Empty tree pe `[]`.
hints: Stack pe nodes push kar root se shuru.
Pop karke visit kar, fir right pehle push (so left pop pehle).
-1 ko skip kar.

title: Atlassian Parentheses Score
description:
Atlassian Confluence editor mein nested formatting ke score calculate karne hain. Balanced parentheses string `s` (sirf `(` aur `)`) di hai. Score rules:
- `()` ka score 1.
- `AB` ka score `A + B` (concatenation).
- `(A)` ka score `2 * A`.

Final score return kar.
hints: Stack pe running scores rakh.
`(` aaye toh stack pe 0 push kar — naya scope.
`)` aaye toh top pop kar — agar 0 hai matlab '()' atomic = 1, warna `2*top`. Add to new top.

title: Postman Leftmost-Minimum Valid Subarrays
description:
Postman API analytics ko batana hai kitne contiguous subarrays mein leftmost element subarray ka minimum hai. Array `a` di hai. Count return kar — subarrays jisme `a[l] <= a[i]` for all `i` in `[l, r]`.

Return integer count.
hints: Har left-index l ke liye, kitne r tak `a[l] <= a[i]` valid rehta hai.
Monotonic increasing stack of indices — jab smaller aaye toh popped index ka 'reach' fix.
Reach = currentIdx - poppedIdx. End mein leftover unke liye reach = n - idx.

title: Microsoft Sum Of Subarray Minimums
description: Microsoft Azure metrics service har contiguous subarray ka minimum nikalke total sum chahti hai. Array `a` diya hai. Return sum of `min(subarray)` over all contiguous subarrays, modulo `10^9 + 7`.
hints: Har element ka contribution = a[i] * (kitne subarrays mein wo minimum hai).
Left distance = previous smaller-or-equal, right = next smaller (strict). Asymmetry duplicates handle karta hai.
Two passes monotonic stacks.

title: PhonePe Stock Span Counter
description:
PhonePe stocks ke daily prices array `prices` di hai. Har din ke liye span calculate kar — consecutive days (current included, going back) jahaan price current day se kam ya equal thi.

Return integer array of spans.
hints: Naive O(n^2) — har day ke liye backwards scan.
Stack of `[price, span]` pairs.
Pop karte ja jab tak top price <= current.

title: Razorpay Stream First Non-Repeating Char
description:
Razorpay anti-fraud system ek stream of characters process karta hai aur har step pe first non-repeating character chahiye. Tujhe string `s` di hai. Process character-by-character — har step ke baad first non-repeating character append kar (ya `#` agar koi nahi).

Return resultant string of length `s.length`.

examples.explanation: After 'a': first NR = 'a'. After 'aa': koi NR nahi = '#'. After 'aab': 'b'. After 'aabc': 'b' still.

hints: Frequency map + queue maintain kar.
Naya char aaye toh queue mein push aur freq increment.
Queue ke front se woh chars hatao jinki freq > 1.

title: Google Binary Grid Max Rectangle
description: Google Maps satellite imagery binary matrix mein largest rectangle of 1s find karna hai. `m` ek 2D array hai of `0`s aur `1`s. Return integer — area of largest rectangle containing only 1s.
hints: Har row ko 'histogram base' treat kar — column-wise heights of consecutive 1s.
Per row pe largest rectangle in histogram solve kar (monotonic stack).
Overall maximum return kar.

title: Uber Trip Fare Smallest Number
description: Uber pricing display ke liye fare ka digit string `num` di hai. Tujhe `k` digits remove karne hain takki bachi hui digits ka number lexicographically smallest ho (leading zeros remove karke). Return string — agar empty ho toh `"0"`.
constraints: num digits string, leading zero ho sakta hai except for '0' itself
hints: Greedy: jaha bhi current digit pichle se chhota mile, pichla hata.
Monotonic increasing stack — agar k bacha hai toh pop kar.
End mein bache k digits tail se hatao. Leading zeros strip karna mat bhul.

title: Paytm Undo Redo Text Editor
description:
Paytm support agent ke text editor mein undo/redo support chahiye. Operations array `ops` di hai — `["type", text]` (current ke baad text append), `["undo"]`, `["redo"]`, `["get"]`. Initial text empty hai.

Return array — type/undo/redo ke liye `null`, get ke liye current text. Naya `type` redo stack clear kar deta hai.

examples.explanation: Type karke 'hi there', undo kar 'hi', redo kar 'hi there' wapas.

hints: Two stacks: `undoSt` aur `redoSt`. Plus current text variable.
type: undoSt.push(cur), cur += text, redoSt clear.
undo: redoSt.push(cur), cur = undoSt.pop(). redo: opposite.
